title: Boat Rental in Belgrade Danube: Costs and Best Charters
html:
To explore the Belgrade Danube, a popular departure point is the Marina Dorcol, where half-day motorboat charters from €250 give access to the river's historic core. BoatHire24 connects you with verified owners and operators for a seamless booking experience.

Where to set off in Belgrade Danube

Begin your journey at Marina Dorcol, just downstream from the Kalemegdan Fortress. Other excellent launch points include the Savski Kej waterfront, the Ada Ciganlija island's marina, and the village of Vinca further east. The Zemun Quay offers a more intimate setting with direct access to the Great War Island. You can rent a boat in a few minutes.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Belgrade Danube?

A budget of €200 to €2,500 per day covers most options, from a two-hour shared tour to a crewed yacht for a full day. Prices vary with boat size, season, and extras such as crew or catering. For an exact comparison of available vessels and current rates, you can rent a boat in Belgrade Danube through BoatHire24's platform. One option worth a look is this motor yacht.

Which boat to rent

  1. Rigid inflatable boat (RIB), ideal for fast sightseeing and exploring narrow channels; up to 6 guests; from €250 for a half-day.
  2. Motor cruiser (e.g. Fairline Targa 43), comfortable for day trips with a cabin and sun deck; up to 8 guests; from €600 for a full day. The Fairline Targa 43 listing on BoatHire24 provides full specifications and availability.
  3. Catamaran (e.g. Lagoon 40), stable and spacious, perfect for larger groups or overnight stays; up to 10 guests; from €1,200 for a full day.
  4. Luxury motor yacht (e.g. Azimut 50), for a premium experience with a skipper and crew; up to 12 guests; from €2,000 for a full day.

Costs of renting a boat in Belgrade Danube

  • Shared 2-hour sightseeing tour: €30, €50 per person, usually from Branko's Bridge.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): €200, €350, suitable for a group of 4, 5.
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours): €800, €1,500, with a skipper and basic amenities included.
  • Crewed luxury yacht full-day (8, 10 hours): €1,500, €2,500, with a professional captain and host.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Belgrade Danube?

For most small powerboats and RIBs under 15 hp, no licence is required, but the operator will confirm exact requirements. Larger motor yachts typically demand an ICC (International Certificate of Competence) or a local Serbian licence. The operator will verify your documentation during booking. You can also see more at browse the listings.

For a less crowded experience, explore the Danube's backwaters near the village of Grocka in early June, before the high season arrives.

At a glance

  • Price band: €200, €2,500 per day (EUR)
  • Peak months: June, July, August
  • Average trip length: 4, 8 hours
  • Typical guest capacity: 4, 10 persons
  • Currency: EUR
  • Local wind: Košava (south-easterly wind, strongest in autumn)

Questions about boat rental in Belgrade Danube

Can I rent a boat in Belgrade Danube without a licence?

Yes, many small motorboats and RIBs under 15 hp can be rented without a licence, but the operator will confirm the exact requirements for each vessel.

What is the best season for boat rental in Belgrade Danube?

The best season is May to September, with July and August offering the warmest weather and regular sailing conditions.

How far can I cruise from Belgrade in a day?

From Marina Dorcol, you can reach Smederevo (40 km downstream) or the Đerdap National Park (150 km) on a full-day itinerary.

Are there guided tours available on the Danube?

Yes, many operators offer guided tours with English-speaking skippers, particularly from the Branko's Bridge departure point.

To start planning your trip, browse the listings on BoatHire24, where you can compare verified boats and book directly with owners for a secure experience. If you own a boat in Belgrade Danube, you can also list your own vessel and reach thousands of potential renters worldwide.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Belgrade Danube and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
